steps.title: Identify Text 2's defense of Asimov's style
steps.content: Text 2 argues that Asimov’s flat characters are not a flaw but a deliberate choice to show that individuals don’t matter in the sweep of history, making his lack of characterization central to his theme.

steps.title: Match the answer to the clue
steps.content: D captures Text 2’s point that Asimov’s minimal characterization is intentional and serves his thematic goal. A, B, and C either misrepresent Text 2’s stance, introduce unrelated comparisons, or contradict its claim. That matches choice D — D is correct.
